description: Port BitWet climbing weather app to Next.js 16 (App Router), TypeScript, React 19, Zustand, and Capacitor (iOS). Pixel-identical design preservation required.

userStories.title: Next.js 16 project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript strict mode, React 19, and basic App Router layout so I can start building.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 16 app created with App Router and TypeScript strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json
React 19 is the configured rendering engine in package.json
ESLint configured
Root layout.tsx exists with html/body structure
Root page.tsx exists with placeholder content
Project builds successfully with `next build`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Port global CSS and theme variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing style.css ported as a global stylesheet so the design is preserved exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: All CSS variables from current style.css ported to src/styles/globals.css
All component styles ported (cards, modals, tabs, buttons, forms, etc.)
All media queries and breakpoints preserved
Dark mode and light mode CSS variable sets both present
globals.css imported in root layout.tsx
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port style.css from project root exactly as-is. Do NOT introduce Tailwind or any component library.

userStories.title: TypeScript types and constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all data types and constants defined in TypeScript so the rest of the codebase has a solid type foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Crag type defined in src/lib/types.ts (id, name, region, lat, lon, alt, rock, orientation, terrain, notes)
RockType union type: 'Granite' | 'Gneiss' | 'Limestone' | 'Sandstone' | 'Conglomerate'
TerrainType union type: 'slab' | 'vertical' | 'overhang'
Orientation union type for cardinal directions
Forecast type defined (hourly data arrays, daily summaries, model source)
ScoreResult type defined (score, label, dryingHours, rockTemp, friction, windShelter)
Score label thresholds defined in src/lib/constants.ts (95+=Send It, 85-94=Atta Boy, etc. down to 0-14=Stay Home)
Rock drying factors defined (Granite 0.6, Gneiss 0.8, Limestone 1.2, Sandstone 2.0)
Season temperature ranges defined matching current implementation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference current data.js and app.js for exact values.

userStories.title: Port crags database to TypeScript
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all 70+ Swiss crags and the 6 default usuals ported to a typed TypeScript module.
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/crags.ts exports all explore crags from current data.js with correct types
Each crag has: name, region, lat, lon, alt, rock, orientation, terrain
Region groupings preserved in src/data/regions.ts (Ticino, Wallis/Valais, Berner Oberland, Zentralschweiz, Jura, Ostschweiz/Graubunden, Gotthard/Uri, Voralpen)
DEFAULT_CRAGS constant exports the 6 default usuals (Sobrio, Chironico Boulder, Salbit Westgrat, Wintersonne, Sonnenplattli, Plattenkreuz)
All coordinate and altitude values match current data.js exactly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy all crag data from data.js verbatim — do not change any values.

userStories.title: Port scoring engine core (computeScoreV2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main scoring function ported to TypeScript with identical behavior to the current vanilla JS.
acceptanceCriteria: computeScoreV2(forecast, dayIndex, crag) implemented in src/lib/scoring.ts
Rain penalty logic identical (up to -25 prob + -35 amount, terrain-adjusted: overhang 70% reduction, slab full)
Temperature penalty logic identical (season-dependent ideal windows, +/-25 max penalty, +3 sweet spot bonus)
Wind penalty logic identical (up to -15, orientation and shelter factor modified)
Severe weather penalties identical (-20 thunderstorm, -12 snow)
Fog penalty identical (altitude-aware, -10 to -1)
Drying time penalty identical (-18 to -1, rock-type adjusted with correct factors)
Dry streak bonus identical (+5 for 5+, +3 for 3+, +1 for 2+ consecutive dry days)
Sunshine bonus/penalty identical (+2 for 8h+, -3 for <2h)
Score clamped to 0-100 range
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the heart of the app. Port line-by-line from current app.js computeScoreV2 function. Zero behavioral changes.

userStories.title: Port scoring helpers (blended, today, weekend, detail metrics)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the blended score, today/weekend score, and detail metric calculations ported.
acceptanceCriteria: blendedScore() averages CH2 and ECMWF scores when both available, falls back to single source
getTodayScore(cragId) finds today's date in forecast and returns blended score
Weekend score logic: combines Saturday + Sunday scores
Drying hours calculation ported (looks back from 9am through hourly precipitation)
Rock temperature calculation ported (air temp + solar gain by orientation - wind cooling)
Friction label calculation ported (cold=good, hot=sweaty based on rock temp)
Wind shelter calculation ported (Sheltered/Partial/Crosswind/Exposed based on wind dir vs orientation)
Wind direction circular mean calculation ported (9am-6pm window)
Trend indicator logic ported (Improving/Getting Worse/Stable)
getScoreLabel(score) returns correct label for 0-100 scale
All functions exported from src/lib/scoring.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference app.js for exact implementations. These functions depend on computeScoreV2 from US-005.

userStories.title: Platform detection utility and PlatformGate component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need platform detection (iOS vs web) and a conditional rendering component for feature-flagging.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/platform.ts exports getPlatform() returning 'ios' | 'web'
Detection uses Capacitor.isNativePlatform() when available, falls back to 'web'
src/hooks/usePlatform.ts exports usePlatform() hook wrapping getPlatform()
src/components/PlatformGate.tsx conditionally renders children based on platform prop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Install @capacitor/core as dependency for platform detection.

userStories.title: Storage adapter abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a storage abstraction layer so localStorage is used now but Supabase can be swapped in later.
acceptanceCriteria: StorageAdapter interface in src/lib/storage.ts with getCrags(), saveCrags(crags), getTheme(), setTheme(theme)
LocalStorageAdapter class implements the interface
Uses key 'bitWet_v1' for crags (backwards-compatible with existing data)
Uses key 'bitWet_theme' for theme preference
Handles missing/corrupted localStorage gracefully (returns defaults)
Exported singleton instance for use by stores
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Zustand crag store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Zustand store for managing the user's saved crags with localStorage persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: src/stores/cragStore.ts exports useCragStore
State: crags array, initialized from StorageAdapter.getCrags() or DEFAULT_CRAGS
Actions: addCrag, editCrag, removeCrag, loadCrags
Every mutation calls StorageAdapter.saveCrags() to persist immediately
New crags get auto-generated id (name + timestamp, matching current logic)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Zustand forecast store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Zustand store for managing weather forecast cache with auto-refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: src/stores/forecastStore.ts exports useForecastStore
State: forecastCache (keyed by crag id), exploreFcCache (keyed by crag id), loading flags
Actions: fetchForecast(lat, lon), fetchAllForecasts(crags), fetchExploreForecast(crags)
Auto-refresh interval of 30 minutes for cached data
Fetches call the internal /api/forecast route handler (not Open-Meteo directly)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Zustand UI store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Zustand store for managing UI state (active tab, sort mode, expanded states).
acceptanceCriteria: src/stores/uiStore.ts exports useUIStore
State: activeTab ('usuals' | 'explore' | 'planner'), currentSort, exploreSort, expandedDays, expandedExplore, theme
Actions: setActiveTab, setSort, toggleDayExpanded, toggleExploreExpanded, setTheme
Theme persisted via StorageAdapter (key 'bitWet_theme')
Respects OS color scheme preference on first load
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Weather API route handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Next.js Route Handler that proxies weather requests to Open-Meteo.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/forecast?lat=X&lon=Y route handler in src/app/api/forecast/route.ts
Fetches from Open-Meteo ICON-CH2 (MeteoSwiss, 5-day, 2km resolution) as primary
Fetches from ECMWF IFS (7-day global) as secondary/backup
Returns both models in response: { best: CH2data, ecmwf: ECMWFdata }
Handles API failures gracefully (returns partial data if one model fails)
Response shape matches what the scoring engine expects (hourly + daily data)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference current fetchForecast() in app.js for exact Open-Meteo API parameters and URL structure.

userStories.title: Geocoding API route handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a geocoding route handler for the Planner's location search.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/geocode?q=X route handler in src/app/api/geocode/route.ts
Proxies to Open-Meteo geocoding API
Returns lat, lon, name for matched locations
Also supports direct coordinate parsing ('47.37, 8.55' format)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Weather icons React components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all SVG weather icons from icons.js converted to React components.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/WeatherIcon.tsx exports a WeatherIcon component taking a weatherCode prop
All weather code SVG icons from current icons.js converted to inline JSX SVGs
Weather code to icon mapping preserved exactly
Weather code to text interpretation mapping exported (e.g., code 3 = 'Overcast')
Icons render at same sizes as current app
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference icons.js in project root for all SVG paths and weather code mappings.

userStories.title: Tabs component and main page layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the tab navigation component and main page shell matching the current app layout.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/Tabs.tsx renders 3 tabs: Usuals, Explore, Planner
Active tab highlighted matching current design
Tab content switches without page reload
Dark/light mode toggle button in header, same position as current app
Main page.tsx uses Tabs component as the shell
Design matches current index.html header and tab bar exactly

Score color coding matches current design exactly (green for high, red for low, etc.)
Score labels shown on hover or alongside score
Edit (pencil) and Remove (X) icons on each card
Loading skeleton shown while forecast is being fetched
Card layout, spacing, fonts match current app pixel-for-pixel

Shows 5 time slots: Sunrise, Morning, Midday, Afternoon, Evening

Displays: drying hours, wind direction + shelter label, rock temperature, friction estimate

Default crags loaded on first visit when no localStorage data exists
'Add Crag' button visible at top of tab

userStories.title: Add/Edit crag modal
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the modal form for adding and editing crags.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/CragModal.tsx renders a modal overlay with form

In edit mode: pre-populates all fields with existing crag data
In add mode: all fields empty, rock defaults to Limestone, terrain to vertical
Submit calls cragStore.addCrag() or cragStore.editCrag()
New crag triggers forecast fetch automatically

userStories.title: Capacitor iOS setup and build
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Capacitor configured so the app builds and runs on iOS.
acceptanceCriteria: Capacitor installed and initialized with iOS platform
capacitor.config.ts configured with correct webDir pointing to Next.js static export
next.config.ts supports output: 'export' for static generation (used by iOS build)
npx cap sync ios runs without errors
iOS project generated in /ios directory
StatusBar and SafeArea handled (no content under notch)
App icon and splash screen configured with placeholders
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The iOS build uses static export. Route Handlers wont work in Capacitor - iOS app should call the deployed Vercel API URL or Open-Meteo directly.

userStories.title: Vercel deployment configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js app ready for Vercel deployment.
